findings.request_body_format: JSON with message.blocks[].text.content containing user prompt
findings.response_format: Streaming newline-delimited JSON objects with op/mask/eventOffset/message structure

analysis.apf_implications: Kimi uses Connect RPC protocol (application/connect+json) over HTTP/2 POST to www.kimi.com. User message is in request body JSON at message.blocks[].text.content. This is NOT WebSocket — standard HTTP POST inspection should work. The domain_patterns in DB should target: kimi.com/apiv2/kimi.gateway.chat.v1.ChatService/Chat
analysis.etap_log_explanation: Previous Etap logs showed small POST bodies (2-934B) because those were ListChats/GetSubscription calls, not the actual Chat request. The Chat POST is ~7.3 kB and contains the full user message.
analysis.classification: INSPECTABLE — standard HTTP/2 POST with JSON body containing user message. Similar to chatgpt/claude pattern but uses Connect RPC instead of SSE.

notes: Kimi chat API fully identified. Connect RPC protocol (gRPC-Web compatible) on www.kimi.com via Cloudflare IPv6 (ICN). No WebSocket. User message clearly present in POST body. Etap can inspect this traffic for APF filtering.
